From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1966 studio album by Eddie Harris

| Mean Greens | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Studio album](/wiki/Album "Album") by [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris") | |
| Released | 1966 |
| Recorded | March 8 & 9 and June 7, 1966 [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Length | 39:18 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records") SD 1453 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Nesuhi Ertegun](/wiki/Nesuhi_Ertegun "Nesuhi Ertegun") |
| [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[The In Sound](/wiki/The_In_Sound_(Eddie_Harris_album) "The In Sound (Eddie Harris album)")* (1965) | ***Mean Greens*** (1966) | *[The Tender Storm](/wiki/The_Tender_Storm "The Tender Storm")* (1967) | | |

***Mean Greens*** is an album by American jazz saxophonist [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris") recorded in 1966 and released on the [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records") label.[[1]](#cite_note-Atlantic_Records_discography-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mean_Greens&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

The [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") review states "Eddie Harris' trademark chops and versatility are well showcased on this respectable follow up to Harris' excellent Atlantic debut, *The In Sound*. *Mean Greens* doesn't have a signature piece, like *The In Sound'*s "Freedom Jazz Dance," but the program of mostly Harris originals is a satisfying set based around two groups".[[3]](#cite_note-Allmusic-3)

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| *[Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic")* | StarStarStar[[3]](#cite_note-Allmusic-3) |

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mean_Greens&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

*All compositions by Eddie Harris except as indicated*

1. "Mean Greens" - 7:27
2. "[It Was a Very Good Year](/wiki/It_Was_a_Very_Good_Year "It Was a Very Good Year")" ([Ervin Drake](/wiki/Ervin_Drake "Ervin Drake")) - 5:08
3. "Without You" - 3:03
4. "Yeah Yeah Yeah" - 5:12
5. "Listen Here" - 3:37
6. "Blues in the Basement" - 8:07
7. "Goin' Home" - 6:44

* Recorded in New York City on March 8, 1966 (track 1), March 9, 1966 (tracks 2–4) and June 7, 1966 (tracks 5–7)

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mean_Greens&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris") - [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone"), [electric piano](/wiki/Electric_piano "Electric piano")
* Ray Codrington - [trumpet](/wiki/Trumpet "Trumpet"), [percussion](/wiki/Percussion "Percussion") (tracks 1, 4–6)
* [Cedar Walton](/wiki/Cedar_Walton "Cedar Walton") - [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano") (tracks 1–4)
* [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") - [organ](/wiki/Electronic_organ "Electronic organ") (tracks 5–7)
* [Ron Carter](/wiki/Ron_Carter "Ron Carter") (tracks 1–4), Melvin Jackson (tracks 5–7) - [bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass")
* [Billy Higgins](/wiki/Billy_Higgins "Billy Higgins") (tracks 1–4), Bucky Taylor (tracks 5–7) - [drums](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit")
* [Ray Barretto](/wiki/Ray_Barretto "Ray Barretto") - [congas](/wiki/Congas "Congas"), [bongos](/wiki/Bongos "Bongos") (tracks 5–7)
